skill.name: grimoire-polymarket
skill.description: Queries Polymarket market data and CLOB state, and manages CLOB orders via the Grimoire venue CLI wrapper backed by the official Polymarket CLI.
skill.summary: Query Polymarket prediction markets and manage CLOB orders through a CLI interface
skill.author: franalgaba

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 100 potential issues, all of which are false positives. The 92 external_commands findings are documentation examples (bash commands in backticks), not actual code execution. The 4 env_access findings reference legitimate API authentication configuration (POLYMARKET_PRIVATE_KEY, etc.) which is standard practice for this type of integration. No malicious intent or security risks identified.

low_findings.title: API Credential Configuration Required
low_findings.description: Skill requires POLYMARKET_PRIVATE_KEY environment variable for authenticated operations. This is legitimate configuration for the Polymarket CLI integration, but users should be aware these credentials are needed for order management features.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Clear documentation of required environment variables for API authentication - standard practice for CLI tools requiring authenticated access.

content.user_title: Query and Manage Polymarket Prediction Markets
content.value_statement: Interact with Polymarket prediction markets directly from Claude. Search markets, query CLOB order books, and manage orders through the official Polymarket CLI wrapper.
actual_capabilities: Search and filter Polymarket prediction markets by query, category, league, sport, or tags
Query CLOB order books, midpoints, prices, and trade history for specific tokens
Retrieve market events, tags, series, and sports metadata
View authenticated CLOB data including orders, trades, balances, and positions
Execute order management operations (create, cancel orders) when authenticated
Run health checks and venue diagnostics via polymarket CLI wrapper
limitations: Requires installation of official Polymarket CLI binary (brew install polymarket)
Wrapper intentionally blocks wallet, bridge, and setup commands for safety
Some passthrough command groups (markets, events, clob, data) are read-only in this wrapper
Market and order data may have rate limits from the underlying Polymarket API

use_cases.title: Market Research
use_cases.description: Search and analyze prediction markets on topics like politics, sports, and current events to inform research or decision-making.

use_cases.title: Trading Operations
use_cases.description: Query order books and manage buy/sell orders on Polymarket CLOB markets through automated Claude workflows.

use_cases.title: Bot Development
use_cases.description: Build automated trading bots or monitoring tools that interact with Polymarket markets through Claude Code or Codex.

prompt_templates.title: Search Markets
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Polymarket for active prediction markets about [topic] and return results in JSON format
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick market discovery on any topic

prompt_templates.title: Query Order Book
prompt_templates.prompt: Get the current order book and midpoint price for token ID [token_id] on Polymarket
prompt_templates.scenario: Check liquidity and pricing before trading

prompt_templates.title: View Positions
prompt_templates.prompt: Show my open orders and current positions for Polymarket market [condition_id]

prompt_templates.title: Market Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: List all active Polymarket markets in the sports category for La Liga, filtered for tradable markets only
prompt_templates.scenario: Find trading opportunities in specific categories

output_examples.input: Search for Bitcoin markets that are active and open for trading
output_examples.output: Returns JSON array of market objects including market ID, question text, current odds, liquidity, expiration date, and trading status for Bitcoin-related prediction markets.

output_examples.input: Get the order book for token ID 0x1234...
output_examples.output: Returns JSON with bids and asks arrays, current midpoint price, spread, and last trade price for the specified token.
Returns JSON list of active orders including order ID, side (buy/sell), size, price, status, and timestamp for all open positions.

best_practices: Always use --format json for programmatic access to market and order data
Run 'grimoire venue doctor --adapter polymarket' before executing trades to verify CLI health
Use search-markets with specific filters (category, league, sport) rather than broad text queries for faster results
Prefer canonical agent commands over legacy compatibility aliases for future-proof workflows
anti_patterns: Do not call Polymarket HTTP APIs directly from advisory tools - always use the CLI wrapper
Avoid using legacy command aliases (market, book, midpoint) - use the canonical clob subcommands instead
Do not hardcode API credentials in prompts or tool calls - use environment variables only
Never attempt to use blocked command groups (wallet, bridge, setup) as they are intentionally disabled

faq.question: Does this skill require Polymarket API credentials?
faq.answer: Authenticated operations like viewing orders and positions require POLYMARKET_PRIVATE_KEY. Market searches and public data queries work without credentials.

faq.question: Can I execute trades directly through this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, when authenticated with API credentials, you can create and cancel orders through the CLOB order management commands. Wallet and bridge operations are blocked for safety.

faq.question: Do I need to install the Polymarket CLI separately?
faq.answer: Yes, you must install the official polymarket binary via 'brew tap Polymarket/polymarket-cli && brew install polymarket' before using this skill.

faq.question: What is the difference between search-markets and markets search?
faq.answer: search-markets is the agent-oriented normalized command with better filtering. markets search is a thinner passthrough to the official CLI with more raw output.

faq.question: Are my API credentials secure?
faq.answer: Credentials are stored as environment variables (POLYMARKET_PRIVATE_KEY, etc.) and only accessed by the underlying Polymarket CLI. This skill does not handle credentials directly.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for automated trading bots?
faq.answer: Yes, but rate limiting and API restrictions may apply. Always test order flows with small sizes first and implement proper error handling for production trading systems.
